book.title: The Goose That Laid the Golden Eggs
book.description: The Goose That Laid the Golden Eggs includes a six-chapter reading sequence, vocabulary, comprehension questions, journal/writing prompts, and assessment checks.

themes: The Goose That Laid the Golden Eggs

weeks.title: The Goose That Laid the Golden Eggs Book Card
vocabulary: 1. subsistence - having just enough to survive; the minimum needed to live
2. luster - a gentle shine or glow
1. authenticity - the quality of being genuine or real
2. consistency - the quality of always behaving in the same way
2. contentedly - in a satisfied and happy way
1. insatiable - impossible to satisfy; always wanting more
2. entitlement - the belief that you deserve something
1. fatally - in a way that causes death or disaster
2. irreversible - impossible to undo or change back
1. magnitude - the great size or extent of something
2. illusory - based on illusion; not real
journal: Write about what the farmer should have done instead of being greedy.
Explain how the ending teaches the lesson of the fable.
writing: Write about what the farmer should have done instead of being greedy.
Explain how the ending teaches the lesson of the fable.
questions: 1. What kind of farmer was Harold?
2. What amazing discovery did Harold make in the goose's nest?
1. What did the goldsmith confirm about the egg?
2. What happened the next morning after Harold found the first golden egg?
1. How did Harold use his wealth?
2. How did Harold treat the magical goose?
1. What dangerous change occurred in Harold's character?
2. How did Harold begin to view his goose?
1. What flawed reasoning led Harold to his terrible decision?
2. What had Harold become blinded by?
1. What did Harold discover when he cut open the goose?
2. What important lesson did Harold learn too late?
assessment: Use chapter comprehension questions for daily checks.
Use journal/writing prompt as written-response evidence.
